raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX my wife was required to go to a mediator as part of the state mandated alternate dispute resolution prior to a family law hearing. The merchant 's website stated the sessions are two hours, during the XXXX  XXXX two hours was discussed, but when the bill came it was for three hours.

Unfortunately, as this was mandated it had to be paid at the time, but I disputed it as soon as I could with XXXX. I sent emails communicating this, or following up with requested information/documentation on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX ( this is where I requested they provide supporting documentation per FTC guidelines ), and most recently on XX/XX/XXXX informing them that they failed to provide documentation as well as notifying them that this complaint would be filed. The only response I get from them now is a statement saying " The amount on the documentation you sent to us is the same as the amount billed to your account ; therefore, we have determined you were billed correctly. '' even though the documentation shows two hours in the agreement, and the paid invoice shows three.
